heroHeadline: Third US Carrier Arrives in Theater; Araghchi to Islamabad as Brent Crude Hits $107 — Ceasefire Extended 3 Weeks
heroSubtitle: On Day 56, the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) entered the CENTCOM area of responsibility — the third US aircraft carrier in theater simultaneously for the first time in more than 20 years — accompanied by an amphibious assault ship carrying ~2,500 Marines and three destroyers. Trump extended the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire by three weeks, but Hezbollah called the extension 'meaningless' and fired rockets at northern Israel within hours of the announcement. Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi began a diplomatic shuttle, traveling to Islamabad for talks with Pakistani FM Dar and Army Chief Munir — with subsequent stops in Muscat and Moscow — in a potential move toward restarting US-Iran negotiations after the collapse of Islamabad Round 2. Brent crude rose to $107.10/bbl as the dual-blockade standoff continued with no resolution in sight. Trump told reporters 'Don't rush me' when asked for a timeline, adding that 'time is not on Tehran's side,' while also clarifying the US would not use nuclear weapons against Iran.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-24. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, NBC News, Euronews, UN News, Axios, Washington Post, Army Recognition, France 24, SOF News, Pakistan Today, Iran Wire, Voice of Emirates.
